item.title: Israel says strike killed new chief of Hamas armed wing in Gaza
item.description: Israel says it killed the new head of Hamas's armed wing in Gaza, Mohammed Odeh, after killing his predecessor in a similar attack earlier this month.

item.title: Iran says U.S. violating ceasefire as Trump seeks "good deal or no deal"
item.description: As Iran accuses the U.S. of a "grave violation" of the fragile ceasefire, Rubio says a deal is still possible despite the latest clash.

item.title: The safest vehicles for teen drivers and how much they cost
item.description: The Insurance Institute and Consumer Reports ranked 96 of the safest cars for teens. Here's what to know.

item.title: Biden sues Justice Department to block release of files from biographer interviews
item.description: Former President Joe Biden has sued the Justice Department seeking to block the release of files related to interviews he conducted with a biographer that later became a central part of a special counsel investigation into his handling of classified documents.

item.title: Middleton wins Texas GOP attorney general runoff over Rep. Roy
item.description: Middleton, currently a State Senator, emerged victorious after a contentious fight.

item.title: Allred defeats Johnson in heated Texas 33 Democratic runoff race
item.description: Former Congressman Colin Allred defeated State Rep. Julie Johnson in Tuesday's runoff to become the Democratic nominee for Texas' 33rd Congressional District.

item.title: Here's what to watch for in the Texas primary runoff election today
item.description: Sen. John Cornyn is projected to lose the GOP Senate primary runoff to Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, who received President Trump's endorsement.

item.title: Minneapolis Police Chief Brian O'Hara resigns after internal probe
item.description: Minneapolis Police Chief Brian O'Hara has resigned following an internal investigation into his conduct, the mayor announced Tuesday evening.

item.title: Galindo projected to lose Texas Dem House runoff after antisemitism accusations
item.description: Maureen Galindo lost to Johnny Garcia after accusations of antisemitism roiled the race in recent days.

item.title: At least 1 dead, 9 missing after chemical tank implosion in Washington
item.description: The damaged tank at Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co. held approximately 900,000 gallons of white liquor, a chemical used in paper processing, authorities said.

item.title: Star NFL running back Josh Jacobs arrested on domestic violence charges
item.description: Green Bay Packers running back Josh Jacobs was arrested Tuesday in Wisconsin on domestic violence charges, officials said.

item.title: Paxton projected to win Texas Senate GOP runoff, defeating incumbent Cornyn
item.description: President Trump had endorsed Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton in the primary runoff.

item.title: 1 killed, 2 survive U.S. strike on boat in Eastern Pacific, SOUTHCOM says
item.description: At least 193 people have been killed in the Trump administration's campaign of missile strikes on boats it claims are trafficking drugs in Latin American waters.

item.title: Ken Paxton beats John Cornyn in Texas GOP Senate primary runoff, CBS News projects | Special Report
item.description: Trump-backed Ken Paxton has defeated Sen. John Cornyn in Texas' GOP Senate primary runoff, CBS News projects. Jessi Mitchell anchored CBS News' special report.

item.title: NASA's moon base plans include landers, buggies and drones for 2028 mission
item.description: Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin​, Astrolab, Lunar Outpost and Firefly Aerospace are awarded with hundreds of millions of dollars in NASA contracts for the first phase of its moon base plans.

item.title: NASA planning 3 moon missions this year in race to build lunar city
item.description: NASA is planning three new moon missions this year as the race to build a lunar city takes shape. NASA officials are hoping to maintain a presence on the south pole of the lunar surface. CBS News space consultant Bill Harwood has more.

item.title: Trump administration targeting lawyers suspected of asylum fraud
item.description: The Trump administration is cracking down on immigration lawyers suspected of improperly filing asylum claims for their clients. In a memo dated Tuesday, the top lawyer for the Department of Homeland Security called on ICE lawyers to increase enforcement. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga has the exclusive.

item.title: Breaking down the U.S. Men's National Team 2026 World Cup roster
item.description: The U.S. has officially unveiled its roster for this year's FIFA Men's World Cup. The U.S., along with Canada and Mexico are the host nations for the tournament, which kicks off next month. CBS News producer Elizabeth Campbell has more from New York City.

item.title: Christian Menefee defeats longtime Democratic Rep. Al Green in Texas House district
item.description: The race pitted two Democratic incumbents against each other after Republicans redrew the congressional map in their favor.

item.title: 5/26: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Trump says his third physical in 13 months went "perfectly"; details still scarce on potential U.S.-Iran peace deal.

item.title: Court gives Mahmoud Khalil more time to fight efforts to deport him
item.description: A federal appeals court is giving former Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil more time to fight the Trump administration's efforts to deport him.

item.title: Diver in Laos miner rescue effort: "We have high hopes that they are still alive"
item.description: "The collapse risks are high because you're constantly touching the roof, and it's hand-dug. There's no support anywhere," said Mikko Paasi, a diver who helped rescue a youth soccer team​ from a cave in Thailand.

item.title: 5/26: CBS Evening News
item.description: At least 1 killed in Washington chemical blast; President Trump has third medical exam of the past year.

item.title: Sen. Ron Johnson on potential deal with Iran: "Let's see how this all develops"
item.description: Sources say a potential U.S.-Iran deal includes a 60-day ceasefire extension, a commitment to halt fighting on every front, including in Lebanon, and assurances from Iran that it won't develop nuclear weapons. Republican Sen. Ron Johnson of Wisconsin joins "The Takeout" to weigh in on the possible agreement, the Justice Department's "anti-weaponization" fund and more.

item.title: Trump administration proposes NDAs for all federal employees to curb leaks
item.description: The Trump administration plans to crack down on press leaks by requiring new and existing federal employees to sign NDAs.

item.title: SPLC seeks dismissal of criminal charges, saying prosecution is vindictive
item.description: The Southern Poverty Law Center asked a federal judge to dismiss the criminal charges filed against it by the Justice Department, saying the indictment represents a "top-down, retributive campaign" directed by President Trump.

item.title: South Carolina Senate rejects Trump's call to redraw congressional map
item.description: The South Carolina Senate has rejected President Trump's push to redraw the state's congressional districts in hopes Republicans could gain an extra seat.

item.title: Court blocks Alabama congressional map, saying state discriminated by race
item.description: The three-judge district court panel ordered Alabama to use a congressional map with two majority-Black districts in the upcoming midterm elections.

item.title: U.S.-wanted nephew of drug lord "El Chapo" is captured in Mexico
item.description: The nephew of notorious Mexican drug lord Joaquin "El Chapo" Guzman is sought by U.S. authorities, officials said.

item.title: Suspect arrested in North Carolina double murder nearly 2 decades later
item.description: The suspect in an infamous North Carolina double murder was arrested after nearly two decades, some 3,000 miles from the scene of the killings, police said.
